items.title: Netflix is getting stingier about its viewing data, and Wall Street isn’t happy
items.description: Netflix’s stock is falling in the wake of mixed earnings and a new plan to cut back on the publication of ‘What We Watched’ reports.

items.title: The U.S. stock market is looking like a rubber band ready to snap
items.description: The S&P 500 has been treading water for more than a month now, but beneath the surface of the U.S. equity market, big changes are taking shape.

items.title: SpaceX’s Starship is set for takeoff — and its floundering stock could use the lift
items.description: The megarocket’s success is SpaceX’s “single most important watch item,” one analyst says.

items.title: Running out of money is not the saddest retirement mistake you can make. This is.
items.description: The saddest financial mistake in retirement has nothing to do with running out of money.

items.title: Is SpaceX’s stock a bust because it fell below $135? Look what happened after Meta’s IPO.
items.description: Close to half of major IPOs sink below their offering price — and stay there for several years. Here’s why SpaceX is no different.

items.title: AST SpaceMobile’s stock is sinking as the SpaceX rival looks to raise more cash
items.description: AST SpaceMobile also pushed back a satellite-launch target.

items.title: Micron has turned into ‘the most important stock in the market.’ So is it time to worry?
items.description: While investors are concerned about a memory peak, analysts say the stock still looks cheap relative to various earnings scenarios.

items.title: Wildfire smoke ‘a growing cardiovascular threat’ as Chicago, Detroit, NYC among world’s most polluted cities today
items.description: “It is a growing cardiovascular threat”: Chicago, Detroit, Minneapolis and New York City are considered some of the world’s most polluted cities Thursday.

items.title: It’s a buyer’s market for bonds
items.description: Bonds are out of fashion with big investors — which makes them an interesting investment opportunity for the rest of us.

items.title: Trump’s media company wants traders to pay to get president’s market-moving social-media posts first
items.description: “Truth API” will be available to institutional customers starting next month.
